learn.title: Hydrogen Fuel Cells in Vehicles
learn.intro: Hydrogen fuel cells let cars and buses run cleanly with only water vapor as exhaust. They cut urban air pollution and ease oil imports. As renewable hydrogen gets cheaper, fuel-cell vehicles offer a zero-carbon option for heavy transport where batteries are less practical.
learn.c1_text: A fuel cell joins hydrogen and oxygen to make electricity. Like a battery that never runs flat, it produces about 0.6–0.8 volts per cell. Think of it as a silent generator: hydrogen in, electrons out, pure water drips from the tailpipe instead of smoke.
learn.c2_text: Stacks of cells raise voltage to power a motor. A Toyota Mirai uses ~330 cells to reach 114 kW. The drivetrain is electric, so acceleration is smooth. Hydrogen refueling takes about 5 minutes, similar to petrol, beating slow battery charging for long trips.
learn.c3_text: India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission with ₹19,744 crore to build clean H2. Pilot fuel-cell buses run in cities like Delhi and Pune. Indian refineries and ports are early hydrogen users, helping cut 50 MMT annual emissions under the mission roadmap.
learn.stat1: Fuel cell vehicles emit only water vapor

learn.title: Real-Time Grid Balancing
learn.intro: Renewables vary with weather, so grids must match supply and demand every second. Smart balancing avoids blackouts and waste. Good integration lets more solar and wind enter the mix, cutting coal use and emissions while keeping lights on for homes and industry.
learn.c1_text: Grid operators keep frequency near 50 Hz by balancing flow. If demand rises, they add generation. Imagine a seesaw: too little power drops frequency, like a slow fan. Plants ramp up within seconds to steady it, preventing damage to appliances and grids.
learn.c2_text: Operators use batteries, hydro and demand response to shift load. A 100 MW battery can inject power in milliseconds. Forecasting solar output 15 minutes ahead helps schedule gas peakers. These tools let grids absorb 30% renewable share without instability.
learn.c3_text: India's national grid spans 800 GW capacity across regions. The POSOCO balances renewable swings daily; solar dips at dusk need 50–70 GW flexible backup. India added 4 GWh battery storage in 2024 to aid real-time balancing of its 200 GW RE fleet.

learn.title: India ISTS Waiver Policy
learn.intro: India's ISTS waiver removes transmission charges for solar and wind sent across states. This slashed renewable costs and unlocked distant clean resources. The policy accelerated auctions, boosted investor confidence and helped India reach record-low tariffs near ₹2/kWh.
learn.c1_text: Inter-State Transmission System charges apply when power crosses state lines. The waiver exempts solar/wind from these fees. Like a toll-free highway for green power, it saved developers ~₹0.30–0.50 per unit, making remote Rajasthan solar competitive in southern states.
learn.c2_text: Waivers cut levelized cost of energy for inter-state projects. A 500 MW solar park saved crores yearly in charges. Combined with reverse auctions, tariffs fell to ₹2.44/kWh in 2022, accelerating 50 GW of cross-state renewable capacity approvals by SECI.
learn.c3_text: MNRE extended the ISTS waiver to June 2025 for solar/wind/hybrid. India's target of 500 GW non-fossil by 2030 relies on such measures. States like Gujarat and Karnataka export surplus clean power, balancing local grids and meeting national climate pledges.

learn.title: Power Purchase Agreements
learn.intro: A PPA is a long-term contract to buy renewable power at fixed rates. It de-risks projects so banks lend money. PPAs drove corporate sourcing worldwide, unlocking finance for gigawatts of solar and wind and speeding the shift from fossil fuels.
learn.c1_text: A PPA is a 15–25 year deal where a buyer pays per kWh from a new plant. Like leasing sunshine: a firm agrees ₹3/unit for 20 years. This steady income lets developers borrow 70% of project cost from banks confidently.
learn.c2_text: Lenders require signed PPAs before funding. A 100 MW solar farm needs ₹400 crore; the PPA assures revenue, lowering interest to ~9%. Without it, risky merchant sales would stall projects. PPAs thus convert policy goals into concrete turbines and panels.
learn.c3_text: SECI signs reverse-auction PPAs with generators, then sells to states. Over 60 GW of solar PPAs were awarded by 2024. Corporate PPAs also grew;Infosys and Tata use them for 100% RE targets, proving the model finances India's clean industrial shift.
learn.stat4: Corporate PPAs cut RE cost risk

learn.title: Solar PV Photoelectric Effect
learn.intro: Solar panels turn sunlight straight into electricity via the photoelectric effect. This clean, silent process powers homes and grids. Understanding it helps us deploy more PV, cutting coal use and emissions while harnessing India's 300 sunny days per year.
learn.c1_text: Sunlight hits silicon cells, knocking electrons loose. Like a pinball machine, photons bump electrons free, creating DC current. One panel of 60 cells makes ~300 W at noon. No fuel, no smoke—just light becoming flow through wired contacts.
learn.c2_text: PV cells have n- and p-type silicon creating an electric field. Electrons cross this junction, producing voltage. Inverters then change DC to 230 V AC for homes. Modern panels hit 20% efficiency, so 5 kW rooftop yields ~20 kWh daily in sunny India.
learn.c3_text: India crossed 90 GW solar in 2024, using this physics at scale. The Bhadla park in Rajasthan (2.2 GW) shows desert PV viability. Rooftop schemes under PM Surya Ghar aim 30 GW by 2027, cutting 50 MMT CO2 via photoelectric generation nationwide.

learn.title: Tidal Stream Energy
learn.intro: Tidal streams use predictable ocean currents to spin turbines, giving steady clean power unlike solar or wind. For coastal nations, this boosts energy security. Though early-stage, it can complement grids with reliable, forecastable megawatts from the sea.
learn.c1_text: Underwater turbines catch tides like windmills in water. Since water is 800x denser than air, a 1 MW tidal unit is small. A 2 m/s current yields ~3 MW from a 20 m rotor. Output follows lunar tides, predictable years ahead, unlike gusty wind.
learn.c2_text: Turbines link via subsea cables to shore inverters. A 10-turbine array can power 4,000 homes at 80% capacity factor. Unlike solar's 20%, tides flow twice daily reliably. Developers monitor corrosion, but mature offshore wind tech eases marine deployment today.
learn.c3_text: India's Gujarat coast holds ~8 GW tidal potential per IRENA-style studies. The Kutch Creek pilot (50 kW) tested currents in 2023. While small vs 200 GW RE, ocean energy can serve island grids like Andaman, cutting diesel use by predictable tidal flow.
